claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period; Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

First, let me read the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now let me help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, **do you have any children that you're responsible for?** If yes, please tell me their names and ages.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 18 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can see that Alex is 18 years old and lives with you at home.

Since Alex is over 16, his eligibility for Child Benefit depends on whether he's in approved education or training. **Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, is he in full-time non-advanced education, or an approved training program?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment. He finished school and went straight into work. He's got a full-time job now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. I need to understand Alex's work situation a bit better.

**How many hours per week does Alex work in his full-time job?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He works full-time - that's about 24 hours or more a week.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me summarize the situation:
- Alex is 18 years old
- He lives with you at home
- He is not in approved education or training
- He works 24 hours or more per week

Based on the official Child Benefit rules, when a child is 16 or over, you can normally still qualify for Child Benefit if they are under 20 and stay in approved education or training. However, you'll stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week and is no longer in approved education or training.

Since Alex is working 24 hours or more per week and is not in approved education or training, you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Complete**

I'm sorry, but you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

**The reason:** While Child Benefit can continue for children aged 16-19 if they remain in approved education or training, it stops when a child starts paid work for 24 hours or more per week and is no longer in education or training. Since Alex is 18, works full-time (24+ hours per week), and is not in approved education or training, you do not qualify for Child Benefit for him.

If Alex's circumstances change in the future (for example, if he were to return to approved education or training and reduce his work hours), you may want to check your eligibility again at that time.

Is there anything else I can help clarify about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and no longer in approved education or training. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, while you can normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 20 if they stay in approved education or training, you will stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week and is no longer in approved education or training. Since Alex works full-time (24 hours or more per week) and is not in approved education or training, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (18 years old), who lives at home but works full-time (24+ hours per week) and is not in approved education or training. Under Child Benefit rules, eligibility ceases when a child aged 16 or over starts paid work for 24 hours or more per week while not in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 18 years old and no longer in approved education or training. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, while you can normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 20 if they stay in approved education or training, you will stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week and is no longer in approved education or training. Since Alex works full-time (24 hours or more per week) and is not in approved education or training, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (18 years old), who lives at home but works full-time (24+ hours per week) and is not in approved education or training. Under Child Benefit rules, eligibility ceases when a child aged 16 or over starts paid work for 24 hours or more per week while not in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.
